$comment: RS brand identity in machine-readable form. Source of truth for the brand validator (Stage alpha). Sync with src/tokens.ts and BRAND.md when values change.

hard_constraints.$comment: Layer 1 — red lines. Render validator rejects any plan that violates these.

colors_allowed.name: primary
colors_allowed.purpose: outro background, lower-third bar, chapter dividers, scene backgrounds (dark)

colors_allowed.name: accent
colors_allowed.purpose: active caption word, keyword highlights, tagline, any positive emphasis

colors_allowed.name: dark
colors_allowed.purpose: transparent overlay over video, caption background (when needed), scene backgrounds

colors_allowed.name: white
colors_allowed.purpose: regular caption words, body text on dark backgrounds

fonts_allowed.purpose: scene titles, chapter dividers, lower-third name, outro tagline, large numbers

sfx.rationale: Synthesized SFX felt distracting in the first reel; reverted by Omar 2026-04-15

numerals.rationale: Western numerals read corporate; Arabic-Indic numerals read religious/traditional

soft_preferences.$comment: Layer 2 — defaults Claude leans toward. Validator warns but does not reject.

lower_third.rationale: Was 820px wide; felt dominant. 640 with auto-height padding feels balanced (Omar feedback 2026-04-25)

scene_layout.rationale: y=900 is the only safe zone above lower-third (1100-1460) and captions (1420). Vertical scenes (process_stepper) handle long Arabic better than horizontal (process_timeline).

emphasis.rationale: Boost the current caption word in place. Floating elements feel busy and compete with captions.

overlays.rationale: y=1200 was too close to captions; y=900 too far. 1080 leaves ~240px clear margin.

smart_zoom.rationale: Zooming on low-confidence faces looks weird; under 90s reels rarely have two windows that pass both gates.

scenes.rationale: Pick every high/medium key_moment that fits 15s spacing. Never artificially cap scene count (Omar feedback).

review_principle.name: review_via_render
review_principle.description: Omar judges animation/visual specs only after watching the render, not by reading JSON
review_principle.implication_for_planner: deliver renders for sign-off; do not ask Omar to validate visuals from the plan alone

creative_freedom_note.$comment: Anything not constrained above is open ground for Claude. Examples (non-exhaustive): scene compositions, animation choreography, pacing & rhythm, hook structure (first 3s), moment selection within the captions, new element types, transitions between scenes, micro-event placement. The only test is: do the hard constraints hold and is the render rated well by Omar?
